features.summary: Deere reported Q4 2024 net income of $1.2B ($4.55 per share) with equipment operations margins of 13.1%. Full-year net income was $7.1B ($25.62 per share) with 18.2% operating margins despite challenging market conditions. The company successfully reduced field inventory levels across key product lines through strategic underproduction. For fiscal 2025, Deere forecasts net income of $5.0-5.5B with equipment operations cash flow of $4.5-5.5B, expecting continued market contraction with demand at or below trough levels.
features.management_outlook: Management expects fiscal 2025 to be challenging with large ag equipment demand in US/Canada down ~30% and construction equipment down 5-15%. The company plans to produce in line with retail demand in most regions after achieving inventory reduction targets in 2024. First quarter 2025 is expected to be particularly weak with sales down 15-20% sequentially and margins 300-400 basis points below full-year guidance due to continued underproduction. Management remains confident in structural improvements, expecting to deliver higher margins at trough than previous peak cycles.
features.risks: Key risks include compressed farm margins from low commodity prices and high input costs, elevated interest rates constraining equipment purchases, and high used equipment inventory levels requiring continued management. Construction markets face headwinds from reduced rental re-fleeting, elevated used inventories, and uncertainty in equipment purchases despite strong underlying construction activity. Geographic mix challenges persist with North American large ag experiencing outsized declines, and European markets facing multi-decade low yields and policy uncertainty.
features.key_drivers: Technology adoption continues driving differentiation with See and Spray covering 1M acres and 75% of combine orders including advanced automation features. The company achieved record engaged acres growth of 20% globally, with highly engaged acres up 30%. Structural cost improvements and disciplined inventory management enabled positive price realization across all segments despite volume declines. Strong dealer network health and targeted pool fund deployment support used equipment inventory reduction while maintaining market position.
